hypothesis: Research infrastructure setup — no code change
lesson: Established research infrastructure: NORTH_STAR.md defines 4 metric categories (20 metrics), EXPERIMENT_POLICY.md defines 10-step iteration cycle, EXPERIMENT_BACKLOG.md ranks 10 experiments with dependency graph. Critical finding: all Phase 1-5 modules (10 files) have unit tests but NONE are wired into server.py or service.py.
next_experiment: EXP-01: Wire concept_taxonomy into recordAttempt to populate concept_ids_json

hypothesis: Calling resolve_concept_ids() inside recordAttempt() will populate concept_ids_json on every attempt event, enabling downstream mastery tracking
lesson: resolve_concept_ids works correctly with existing TOPIC_TAG_TO_CONCEPT mapping. skill_tags like fraction/decimal/volume map cleanly to concept_ids. Pure data enrichment with 0 regressions. Foundation ready for mastery_engine and error_classifier wiring.
next_experiment: EXP-03: Wire error_classifier into recordAttempt to populate error_type column

hypothesis: Calling classify_error() on incorrect attempts will populate error_type column, enabling error-pattern analysis
lesson: classify_error works correctly with keyword-only args. correct_answer from extra dict, duration_sec from duration_ms/1000, changed_answer from extra. For incorrect answers, error_type values: guess_pattern, stuck_after_hint, careless, concept_misunderstanding. Correct answers get None. 0 regressions.

hypothesis: Calling update_mastery() after each attempt for each resolved concept_id will maintain live la_student_concept_state rows, enabling adaptive item selection
lesson: update_mastery takes StudentConceptState + AnswerEvent (dataclass), returns (updated_state, MasteryActions). get_concept_state returns default UNBUILT state if no row exists. upsert_concept_state accepts conn= kwarg for shared transaction. Wiring 4 modules (concept_taxonomy, error_classifier, mastery_engine, concept_state) because mastery_engine depends on concept_state CRUD. All pure, no regressions.

hypothesis: Exposing get_all_states via GET /v1/student/concept-state will enable frontends and dashboards to display concept mastery data
lesson: Read-only endpoint wiring is trivially safe. Import learning_get_all_concept_states in try block, add GET endpoint with auth+student verification, serialize states to dict. No write side effects.

hypothesis: Surfacing remediation_needed and calm_mode from MasteryActions in recordAttempt response enables frontend to trigger remediation UI when student struggles
lesson: MasteryActions already computes remediation_needed (consecutive_wrong>=3) and calm_mode_entered. Just surface them in each mastery entry and aggregate remediation_concepts list. Zero new logic needed - the mastery engine does the computation.

hypothesis: Wiring check_unlocks and compute_badges into recordAttempt enables frontend to show zone/boss unlocks and earned badges in real-time after each answer
lesson: check_unlocks and compute_badges are pure functions taking List[StudentConceptState]. After mastery loop, call get_all_states to collect all concept states, then pass to both functions. Serialize results as dicts in response. No DB writes needed - gamification is purely computed from mastery state.

hypothesis: Exposing generate_teacher_report via GET /v1/teacher/classes/{class_id}/concept-report enables teachers to view concept-level mastery distribution, blocking concepts, and at-risk students
lesson: Teacher report endpoint follows same pattern as concept-state endpoint. Import generate_teacher_report + report_to_dict + get_class_states in try block. Get class student IDs from class_students table, call get_class_states, convert dict-of-dicts to dict-of-lists, generate report, serialize.

hypothesis: Exposing generate_parent_concept_progress via GET /v1/student/concept-progress enables parents to view child concept mastery with Chinese labels, encouragement, and markdown-formatted progress
lesson: Parent report enhanced follows same pattern as teacher report. Import generate_parent_concept_progress + progress_to_dict. Get concept states via get_all_concept_states, pass to generate function. States dict values() gives List[StudentConceptState].
next_experiment: EXP-04: practice/next endpoint or EXP-10: fix pre-existing failures

hypothesis: Exposing select_next_item via POST /v1/practice/concept-next enables frontends to request adaptive concept-level recommendations based on mastery state
lesson: Build virtual QuestionItem pool from CONCEPT_TAXONOMY (3 items per concept: easy/normal/hard). select_next_item picks optimal concept+difficulty. Careful not to move fields between Pydantic models when inserting new class. F=3 initially from PracticeNextRequest losing topic_key field.

hypothesis: Fixing 5 pre-existing test failures across 5 files will establish a clean green baseline with 0 deselections
lesson: Three root causes: (1) hardcoded timestamps drifting outside analytics 30-day window  fix with datetime.now()-timedelta, (2) engine module not reloaded after env var change  fix with importlib.reload(engine), (3) HTML template assertions checking literal values instead of template variables  fix with semantic assertions.
next_experiment: AutoResearch campaign complete  all 10 experiments green, D5=10/10

hypothesis: Adding get_hint_effectiveness_stats() to analytics.py enables measuring hint success rate, stuck-after-hint rate, by-level distribution, and by-concept breakdown from existing la_attempt_events data
lesson: Hint data (hints_viewed_count, hint_steps_viewed_json, la_hint_usage) was already being collected since Phase 1, but no aggregation function existed. Adding a pure read-only analytics function with zero schema changes is the lowest-risk way to enable measurement. concept_ids_json can contain multiple concepts per attempt, so by_concept counts may double-count.
next_experiment: EXP-A2: Expose hint effectiveness via API endpoint

hypothesis: Exposing get_hint_effectiveness_stats via GET /v1/student/hint-effectiveness enables teacher/parent dashboards to display hint quality data
lesson: httpx.ASGITransport pattern works well for endpoint tests. Optional student_id enables per-student and class-wide queries in one endpoint.

hypothesis: Adding format_hint_summary_for_teacher() to teacher_report.py produces Chinese summaries with risk flags and recommendations, completing Direction A
lesson: Chinese-language summaries with risk thresholds provide actionable guidance. Sorting by success_rate ascending puts weakest areas first. Min attempt threshold prevents false flags.
next_experiment: EXP-B1: Mastery transition edge case audit

hypothesis: Auditing mastery state transitions against edge cases reveals scoring issues and documents behavior via 12 unit tests
lesson: Direct unit testing of update_mastery() far more effective than integration-only testing. Gate-based promotion is robust. REVIEW_NEEDED blocking is by design.

hypothesis: Adding targeted tests for transfer, delayed review, remediation, calm mode, too-slow, and full lifecycle paths ensures complete mastery engine coverage
lesson: Transfer and delayed review bonuses stack with correct deltas. Remediation and calm_mode share threshold, always trigger together. REVIEW_NEEDED re-promotes via same gate.

hypothesis: Adding mastery distribution analytics to concept-report gives teachers actionable class-level mastery overview
lesson: StudentConceptState .mastery_score and .mastery_level make distribution computation straightforward. Score histogram 5 buckets map to mastery level boundaries.

hypothesis: Adding Chinese labels and _pct companions to teacher report fields improves readability and consistency with hint_summary pattern
lesson: Dual raw+pct pattern from hint_summary is gold standard. _RISK_LEVEL_ZH centralizes translation. struggling_concepts changed from [str] to [dict].

hypothesis: A compact one-page Chinese summary lets teachers scan class status in 30 seconds
lesson: Place one_page_summary LAST in enrichment chain to consume all prior enrichments. Chinese f-string formatting works well for structured summary.

hypothesis: Adding severity classification, pattern description, and actionable recommendations to blocking concepts enables teacher decision-making
lesson: Severity thresholds 70%/40% map well to teacher intuition. Wrapping enrichment inside report_to_dict is transparent. Simple count comparisons effective for pattern detection.

hypothesis: Replacing random concept selection with score-aware prioritization (closest-to-promotion for developing, stalest-first for review, highest-score for approaching) improves selection quality
lesson: Minimal code change (random.choicemin/max with key) yields deterministic, score-aware concept selection. Passing concept_states to strategy functions enables data-driven decisions.
